title: knautia arvensis var. longifolia
intro: Knautia arvensis var. longifolia, also known as Long-stalked Field Boneset, is a variety of flowering plant in the phlox family that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. Long-stalked Field Boneset can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
